data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) stock surges after NVIDIA's (NVDA) AI-driven earnings rise. CRWV gets $4B for new data center; NVDA Q2 revenue hits $46.74B, up 56%.
data.description: CRWV stock surged on Thursday following the news of NVIDIA Corp.'s AI-driven earnings. The company announced a significant $4 billion investment into a new data center in Pennsylvania, contributing to the positive momentum. Additionally, a series of bullish analyst upgrades further propelled the stock's growth. NVDA's Q2 earnings revealed a revenue of $46.74 billion, marking a 56% increase year-over-year, with notable growth of 17% in Blackwell data center revenue compared to the previous quarter. This development suggests a promising future for CRWV stock as it continues to climb steadily. For more detailed information, refer to the full article available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: RFK Jr. causing chaos at CDC by pushing to fire Director Susan Monarez. Lawyers say only President Trump can resolve impasse.
data.description: Attorneys representing Susan Monarez, the head of the agency, argue that the standoff with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. can only be settled by President Trump.

data.title: TNF Pharmaceuticals (Nasdaq: TNFA) announces 1:100 reverse stock split for compliance, to begin trading Tuesday.
data.description: TNF Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: TNFA) based in New York, a company focusing on treatments for autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, has approved a reverse stock split. This split will consolidate the company's common stock at a ratio of 1 post-split share for every 100 pre-split shares. Trading of TNF's common stock on a split-adjusted basis will commence on Tuesday, S.

data.title: Morgan Stanley predicts Fed rate cuts to start in September after Powell's Jackson Hole speech, with quarterly cuts through 2026, reaching a terminal rate of 2.75% to 3.00%.
data.description: Morgan Stanley predicts that the Federal Reserve will begin lowering interest rates in September, changing its previous forecast of maintaining rates until March 2026. This shift follows Fed Chair Jerome Powell's remarks at the Jackson Hole conference, emphasizing addressing labor market challenges over inflation targeting. The bank anticipates a 25 basis-point rate reduction in September, with another cut in December. They project quarterly decreases until 2026, reaching a final rate of 2.75% to 3.00%. The report suggests a potential positive impact on the housing market. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Aurora and McLeod Software partner to create industry's first TMS for self-driving trucks, enhancing user experience. $AUR #autonomoustrucking
data.description: In a joint announcement, Aurora (NASDAQ: AUR) and McLeod Software disclosed a strategic partnership to develop the initial Transportation Management System (TMS) specialized for autonomous trucks. This collaboration aims to enhance the user experience for customers leveraging Aurora's self-driving trucks, ultimately driving the adoption of autonomous trucking within the industry. Both organizations, renowned in their fields, aspire to revolutionize the transportation sector through this innovative partnership.

data.title: Teledyne (NYSE: TDY) to acquire TransponderTech from Saab AB, includes AIS, VDES, GNSS technologies.
data.description: Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE: TDY) has revealed its plans to acquire the TransponderTech business from Saab AB, based in Sweden. This acquisition comprises a range of connected commercial maritime products such as Automatic Identification System (AIS), VHF Data Exchange System (VDES), and Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) technologies. The deal solidifies Teledyne's position in the industry, adding valuable assets to its portfolio.

data.title: BILL Holdings (NYSE: BILL) surpasses Q4 earnings with 53 cents per share, beating analyst estimates of 41 cents. Revenue at $383.34M exceeds Street estimate of $376.17M, up from $343.66M last year. Visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: Bill Holdings, Inc (NYSE: BILL) exceeded expectations with its fourth-quarter results, announced after the market closed on Wednesday. The company reported earnings of 53 cents per share, surpassing the analyst projection of 41 cents. Quarterly revenue stood at $383.34 million, also beating the Street's estimate of $376.17 million and marking an increase from the $343.66 million recorded in the same quarter last year. CEO stated, "Fiscal year 2025 was a significant period for BILL, marked by growth, profitability, and the launch of new software and payment products for customers and suppliers, expanding our market reach. Our Q4 performance underscores our competitive edge, with around half a million SMBs and 9,000 accounting firms utilizing our platform."

data.title: Tracfone introduces 1st unlimited plan, Tracfone Freedom, offering talk, text, data for $45/month. Available at tracfone.com.
data.description: Tracfone Wireless has introduced Tracfone Freedom, a new mobile plan offering unlimited talk, text, and data at $45 per month with Auto-pay. This plan is the company's first unlimited data option and aims to meet the increasing need for affordable and dependable connectivity. Customers can access this exclusive single-line plan on tracfone.com.

data.title: Inspira gets U.S. patent for ART500, to dominate $20B market with low flow oxygenation system. 16 novel claims made.
data.description: Inspira™ Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd., a leading company in life-support and diagnostic technologies based in Ra'anana, Israel, has recently secured U.S. Patent approval for its low flow rates extracorporeal oxygenation system and methods. This patent is crucial as it covers the core technology of their ART500 device, a valuable asset in their plan to tap into the $20 billion advanced respiratory support market. The innovation included in the patent consists of 16 unique claims, highlighting its novelty and importance in the industry.

data.title: T-Mobile US, Inc. executives will present at two investor conferences on Thursday, Sep 4, 2025, at 9:40 am EDT.
data.description: T-Mobile US, Inc. revealed that key executives will participate in investor conferences on Thursday, September 4, 2025. Peter Osvaldik, the CFO, and Jon Freier, the President of the Consumer Group, will share a business update at the Bank of America Securities Media, Communications & Entertainment Conference at 9:40 a.m. EDT. Additionally, Srini Gopalan, the COO, is expected to participate in another session during the same event. The company's stock trades on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol TMUS.

data.title: Nasdaq rises with Pure Storage up 30%; Nvidia cuts losses. Dow slips post surprise GDP and jobless claims.
data.description: On Thursday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline in a day of varied trading, influenced by unexpected GDP figures and jobless claims. Nvidia shares fell post its latest earnings report. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq saw an increase with Pure Storage surging by 30%, leading to a breakaway buy signal. Nvidia managed to reduce its losses during the day's trading activity.

data.title: Loman AI secures $3.5M funding to enhance restaurant operations using Voice AI, leading to 22% revenue growth.
data.description: An Austin-based startup focused on voice AI technology, Loman, has successfully secured a $3.5 million seed funding round. The company achieved this milestone after handling a large volume of restaurant calls across the nation. Through their innovative technology, Loman was able to help restaurant operators increase their revenue by an impressive 22%.
